items.summary: Arsenal extended their Premier League lead to seven points over Manchester City after Bukayo Saka's deflected strike secured a hard-fought 1-0 victory at Brighton, with the Gunners keeping their quadruple dream alive.

source.title: 'I feel blessed, I feel joy' – Saka revels in decisive goal on 300th Arsenal appearance

items.summary: Saka celebrated his 300th Arsenal appearance by scoring the winner at Brighton, becoming the fourth-youngest player in club history to reach that milestone behind David O'Leary, Cesc Fabregas and Liam Brady.

source.title: 'I feel blessed, I feel joy' – Saka revels in decisive goal on 300th Arsenal appearance

items.summary: Norway manager Ståle Solbakken has assured fans that captain Martin Ødegaard's knee injury is not serious, with the midfielder expected to return in late March or April as Arsenal prepare for their FA Cup fifth-round tie against League One side Mansfield Town.

source.title: Arsenal Receive Crucial Injury Update About Key Player
